Furthermore, it might call the custom based integration flow “Step 2b - Interchange Processing Flow - Base-Overlay Mapping Process“, if a base overlay-based mapping process is required. How this works, will be explained in another blog soon.
  • B2B Integration Factory - Interchange Assembly Flows: Once the message payload is correctly mapped and processed into the appropriate target message type based on the required target, it is necessary to fill the type specific header of this payload with the header specific data that are configured in the Trading Partner Agreement (TPA). Based on the supported type system, there is an individual assembly process flow located in the integration package: “B2B Integration Factory - Interchange Assembly Flows”.
  • Step 3 - Receiver Communication Flow: The finally assembled receiver interchange payload will be handed over to the interchange flow “Step 3 - Receiver Communication Flow” which is responsible to send this payload to the final target system via either one of the supported communication protocols or via the additionally supported communication protocols.
  • B2B Integration Factory - Communication Receiver Flows (Custom): Especially the communication receiver flows of the non-supported communication flows are in the integration package “B2B Integration Factory - Communication Receiver Flows (Custom)”. You will find some examples in here for example EMail.receiver and SFTP.receiver. There is also a template available, which can be used for the creation of some further own communication receiver flows.

 

Released Extensions

The following table shows you the list of extensions in this release. You’ll find further details in the documentation that is provided with the Integration Packages in the Business Accelerator Hub’s Community section.

Extension

Current Standard Implementation

What is possible by this extension?

Syntax Type and Type System related Routing

Just EDI (UN/EDIFACT, ASC X12, TRADACOMS) syntax representation in sender message payloads is supported via TPM.

It allows you to dynamically add further syntax representations such as JSON, CSV, etc. so that these can be supported at sender side, too.

Split of Sender Payloads with Several EDI Interchanges

The TPM system just work with a single interchange (UN/EDIFACT UNB … UNZ, or ASC X12 ISA … IEA) per sender message payload.

It allows you to split into several interchanges, if there are several in the sender message payload.

Modification of EDI extracted key values

Just the extracted values from the supported extraction rules will be used for calculating the PID. This might lead to mismatches.

The possibilities of this extraction value mapping are so extensive that the details of the extraction value mapping are described in a separate blog.. 

Selection of XML based Type Systems

Just the XML type systems: SAP SOAP, SAP IDOC, and GS1XML are supported at sender side.

It allows you to add further XML type systems at sender side.

Extraction of key fields of XML or Flat based Sender Interchange Payload

Just extracts predefined key fields for TPM supported type systems only.

It allows you to define your own rules for extracting key fields, and this is also possible for additional, non-supported type systems.

Further processing of received functional acknowledgement interchanges

It is not possible to further process received functional acknowledgements so that e.g a mapped SAP IDOC SYSTAT message with the status from the functional acknowledgement will be submitted to the S/4HANA OnPremise system.

It allows to enable a further processing of the received functional acknowledgement so that e.g. a SAP IDOC SYSTAT message can be mapped from it and submitted to the connected SAP S/4HANA OnPremise system.

Pass Through

Does not support the simple handover of sender interchange message payloads.

It allows you to simply pass through sender interchanges to the receiver without changes in the payload itself, but with complete monitoring in the B2B Monitoring.

Sender EDI Interchange Custom Processing

EDI Splitter requires very specific content. Therefore, a slight modification of the header part in the sender interchange might be necessary. This is not supported yet.

It allows modifying the sender interchange payload in front of the EDI splitter via an additional custom integration flow.

Disable Sender EDI Interchange Syntax Validation

The syntax validation provided by the EDI Splitter is always enabled.

It allows to disable the syntax validation per custom parameter in a Business Transaction Activity.

B2B Monitoring - Business Document Split Event

The B2B Monitoring does not show specific entries for the interchange and per split message.

It enables to display one B2B Monitoring entry for the sender interchange payload and separate B2B Monitoring entries per split EDI message.

Sender XML Interchange Split Processing

Just splits EDI (ASC X12, UN/EDIFACT) sender interchange payloads, but not XML based type systems.

It also allows to split XML based type systems.

Set Receiver Interchange Control Numbers

It is not possible to individually set different control numbers for the interchange receiver payload and for the functional group inside of this interchange.

It allows to individually set different control numbers for the interchange and the functional group per Business Transaction Activity.

Customized Mapping Process

The current custom mapping process also requires the presence of syntactically correct source and target MIG. Custom mapping means a mapping, which is not created by the Integration Advisor.

It allows to enable custom mapping with just dummy MIGs, because the current release of the TPM requires the assignment of some source and target MIGs.

Returning Functional Ack. Interchange Modification

Just generates for sender interchanges with a functional acknowledgement required the predefined functional acknowledgement as supported by the EDI Splitter

It allows to define your own customizations and extensions in the already generated functional acknowledgement by the EDI Splitter.

Receiver Interchange Gather Bulk Processing

It does not gather split messages which belong together in an assembly of the receiver interchange.

It allows to gather messages that are split by the same transaction process so that these can be assembled for the receiver interchange.

Receiver Interchange Assembly Processing

It just enables the assembly and the insertion of just standard TPM configured content into the interchange/message header of the receiver interchange for the TPM supported type systems.

It allows to define own assembly flows with their own assembly rules and TPM configuration data insertion rules. This allows to set assembly flows for additional type systems.

XML to Other Syntax Conversion

It just provides the XML to EDI or vice versa conversion whereby the internal processing syntax is XML.

It allows to add further syntax conversions such as XML to CSV.

Toggle Sender- and Receiver-Name in Returning Functional Acknowledgement Interchange

The B2B Monitoring does not show the sender and receiver name in the correct places for generated and submitted functional acknowledgements. These must be toggled.

It toggles the sender and receiver names in generated functional acknowledgements so that these will be correctly shown in the B2B Monitoring.

Step 3 - Receiver Communication Flow - Extended → Integration Process: AS2 Receiver Channel and (SFTP) Receiver Channel

The AS2 receiver communication channel does not provide a possibility to set an expression for getting final names according to this expression.

It allows to set an expression for file names so that these will be dynamically generated via this expression. This expression can be set via custom parameter in each Business Transaction Activity.

Set Custom Search Attributes (with multiple values)

Just custom search attributes with one value can be set. For example, the business document numbers of several IDOC documents in one IDOC message can’t be collected.

It allows to set a list of values per search attribute.

Base-Overlay Approach

A Base-Overlay-Approach is not support. What is a Base.Overlay-Approach? Stay curious, because there will be a blog soon which explains the Base-Overlay Approach

It allows to enable the Base-Overlay-Approach.

 

How to get the B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages?

If you use the TPM and you need one of the extensions above, we recommend to copy all B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages into your own tenant. For this, there are two options:

  • Via the SAP Business Accelerator Hub in where you can discover and view this content.
  • Via Discovery tab in your own SAP Integration Suite tenant, from where you can copy the integration packages into your own workspace.

 

Via SAP Business Accelerator Hub

You can discover the B2B Integration Factory – Integration Packages in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and add the integration packages into your tenant workspace via the following steps:

1.) Go to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

  1. Go to More and
  2. Select Community
  3. Click View All Community at the bottom of this section

 

 

 


 

2.) If you see the whole list of community items

  1. Search for “B2B Integration Flows”
  2. Then you’ll just see the B2B Integration Factory - Integration Packages with the mentioned extensions.

 

 

3.) In each integration package tile

  1. You’ll find a button “Add to Workspace” for adding this content into your tenant workspace.
  2. This will open a new window “Add to workspace” in whereyou should agree to the terms of use of community content and click on “Confirm” button.Follow the instructions as described on the SAP Help page : Add a Package To a Workspace.
  3. Once the content is added to your workspace, follow the further installation and configuration instructions that you’ll find under Documents --> In the Configuration Guide - B2B Integration Factory - Trading Partner Management

 

 

Via your SAP Integration Suite Tenant

Alternatively, you can directly obtain the B2B Integration Packages in your SAP Integration Suite tenant via the following steps:

1.) You go in your SAP Integration Suite tenant to Discover à Integrations

  1. Search for “B2B Integration Factory”
  2. You will see all the mentioned integration packages in the overview list.

 

2.) Open each “B2B Integration Factory – xx” integration package

  1. Click on button “Copy” for copying it into your tenant workspace.
  2. Follow the further instructions that you’ll find under Documents --> in the Configuration Guide - B2B Integration Factory - Trading Partner Management
